title: --x-initial-edge triggers unwanted edge on first model eval
body:
Hi,

--x-initial-edge triggers unwanted edge on first model eval.
Happen in verilator upstream Verilator 5.015 devel rev v5.014-11-g6c8045726
Doesn't happen in Verilator 4.218 2022-01-17 rev v4.218

Toplevel : 
```verilog
module top
  (input logic clk);

   always_ff @ (posedge clk) begin
     assert(0); //Used as a way to know if posedge clk was triggered
   end
endmodule
```

Test
```c++ 
    Vtop* top = new Vtop; 
    //This eval will trigger always_ff @ (posedge clk) when --x-initial-edge is used (it shouldn't' ?)
    top->eval(); 
    top->final();
```

You can find in [example-systemverilog.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/12356677/example-systemverilog.tar.gz) a way to reproduce : 
`make clean run`

Got :

```
-- RUN ---------------------
obj_dir/Vtop
[0] %Error: top.v:12: Assertion failed in TOP.top: 'assert' failed.
%Error: top.v:12: Verilog $stop
Aborting...
make: *** [Makefile:90: run] Aborted (core dumped)
```

Expected :

```
-- RUN ---------------------
obj_dir/Vtop

-- DONE --------------------
```


Tested on Debian.

The main issue i have with this upstream behaviour, is that clocked assertion are getting triggered at the first eval, without necessarly having a change to reset the system first.

Thanks ^^
